content:
I'm stuck on updating this and other C structs which worked on v6.14, but doesn't work on Linus' current tree (with rust-next):

unsafe impl init::Zeroable for bindings::drm_gem_shmem_object {}

Changing init to pin_init didn't work. I'm getting E0117.

What's the new way to do this? @Benno Lossin ?
I think we should put those kinds of things into the bindings crate itself, but not 100% sure.
cc @Miguel Ojeda

Is that the orphan rule? Then, yeah, one way would be putting them there. Alternatively, could we use a transparent wrapping type?

If we are going with the bindings approach, then I think ideally we would have bindgen annotate things like this automatically. Could we perhaps use --with-derive-custom to annotate those?
I would think Zeroable for C structs would be the rule rather than exception.
Is there a way to make Zeroable not foreign (as it wasn't in 6.14 I guess since this worked then)?

Miguel Ojeda said:

Is that the orphan rule? Then, yeah, one way would be putting them there. Alternatively, could we use a transparent wrapping type?

Yeah it's the orphan rule. A transparent wrapper type is probably very annoying and won't scale. (If the bindings type is only used in Opaque, then there isn't a problem, as Opaque already should implement Zeroable. if it doesn't, it's an easy fix)

If we are going with the bindings approach, then I think ideally we would have bindgen annotate things like this automatically. Could we perhaps use --with-derive-custom to annotate those?

Yeah, I think such an option would be nice (after all, we have the derive(Zeroable) macro). Maybe we need a derive(MaybeZeroable) that doesn't error when it can't implement Zeroable due to not all fields being Zeroable (IIRC the normal one does error).

Rob Herring said:

Is there a way to make Zeroable not foreign (as it wasn't in 6.14 I guess since this worked then)?

I moved all of the items that belong to pin-init into their own crate for better maintainability, see https://lore.kernel.org/all/20250308110339.2997091-1-benno.lossin@proton.me

I don't think there is a way to make it not foreign, as the pin-init crate also depends on it.

We asked the rust folks about relaxing the orphan rule, but there hasn't been a lot of progress there.

For the moment, if we do the impls manually in rust/bindings/lib.rs as Benno suggested, then I guess we will need:

diff --git a/rust/Makefile b/rust/Makefile
index 2a8342e9c5b5..33eb3d0c55af 100644
--- a/rust/Makefile
+++ b/rust/Makefile
@@ -501,9 +501,10 @@ $(obj)/ffi.o: private skip_gendwarfksyms = 1
 $(obj)/ffi.o: $(src)/ffi.rs $(obj)/compiler_builtins.o FORCE
        +$(call if_changed_rule,rustc_library)

-$(obj)/bindings.o: private rustc_target_flags = --extern ffi
+$(obj)/bindings.o: private rustc_target_flags = --extern ffi --extern pin_init
 $(obj)/bindings.o: $(src)/bindings/lib.rs \
     $(obj)/ffi.o \
+    $(obj)/pin_init.o \
     $(obj)/bindings/bindings_generated.rs \
     $(obj)/bindings/bindings_helpers_generated.rs FORCE
        +$(call if_changed_rule,rustc_library)

And then Rob can write e.g.

diff --git a/rust/bindings/lib.rs b/rust/bindings/lib.rs
index 014af0d1fc70..5553056a66ab 100644
--- a/rust/bindings/lib.rs
+++ b/rust/bindings/lib.rs
@@ -55,3 +55,5 @@ mod bindings_helper {
 }

 pub use bindings_raw::*;
+
+unsafe impl pin_init::Zeroable for drm_gem_shmem_object {}

If that works for you, Rob, for the moment, then that is great, and we can work on perhaps automating it better in the future (or discussing a way with upstream Rust to do this better).

If it does not work for you, could you please give us your branch so that we play with it?
I will try this on the gem shmem branch, since this move unfortunately broke both that and the kms bindings

if it works there could we get this into a patch so we could get this onto the mailing list? Just want to make sure this isn't going to block work since Rob is right: zeroable structs are the rule in C rather than exceptions
also - this solution doesn't work

Lyude said:

also - this solution doesn't work

It compiled for me, it must... Or maybe it didn't with the other errors I'm still sorting out.
@Rob Herring wait are you working on the gem shmem branch right now?
What branch? I'm rebasing tyr to 6.15 with latest nova branch.
Gotcha. sorry - I forgot it's not a branch as well but a patch series, I've just been here trying to rebase the patches that daniel sent for gem shmem support in rust
I'd like a branch with everything as we discussed a while back...
tbh I dont remember much of that conversation :S, would have to ask danilo about that. I've been kind of focused on trying to get all of these dependencies upstream

https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/lyudess/linux/-/commit/040c3a2b34e4dd48d2a25133e46f40c1f60100de is the workaround that I have on my branch right now that doesn't seem to work. It complains that Zeroable is a safe trait which, doesn't really make any sense since it's not
You need to drop 'bindings::' I think
doesn't seem to work either...

i'm unconvinced this isn't just rust lsp being broken so I'm going to ignore it for the time being and come back if it ends up still being a problem.
would be really nice to get this fixed upstream though since this is going to be a problem for quite a lot of bindings

i'm unconvinced this isn't just rust lsp being broken

Do you mean it compiles fine and it is just rust-analyzer complaining? IIRC I built the diff above and it seemed fine.

What if we add #[derive(MaybeZeroable)] onto every struct in bindings? Ie after invoking bindgen do some sed to add the above derive.

the MaybeZeroable derive macro will then only implement Zeroable if all fields implement Zeroable.
we can ask bindgen to provide the --with-derive-custom Miguel suggested above, but we probably want to have a solution now
yeah it might be - I'm trying to clean up the rest of this code so i can actually figure that out today

Benno Lossin said:

What if we add #[derive(MaybeZeroable)] onto every struct in bindings? Ie after invoking bindgen do some sed to add the above derive.

the MaybeZeroable derive macro will then only implement Zeroable if all fields implement Zeroable.

I think this sounds good.
BTW though - I think bindgen does provide a way to ask it to derive a trait for a specific type, I think I use it once or twice in the KMS bindings
:man_facepalming: I misread Miguel's message above and didn't realize that the option already existed, I'll add it to my list of patch ideas, maybe I get to sooner, since it's simple :)
got some good news for you @Lyude & @Rob Herring : https://github.com/y86-dev/linux/commits/bindgen-zeroable/

:sweat_smile: ah not so fast:

error[E0277]: the trait bound `bindings::qspinlock__bindgen_ty_1: pin_init::Zeroable` is not satisfied
   --> rust/kernel/lib.rs:246:23
    |
246 |     assert_zeroable::<bindings::drm_gem_shmem_object>();
    |                       ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ the trait `pin_init::Zeroable` is not implemented for `bindings::qspinlock__bindgen_ty_1`
    |
    = help: the following other types implement trait `pin_init::Zeroable`:
              ()
              (A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I, J)
              (B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I, J)
              (C, D, E, F, G, H, I, J)
              (D, E, F, G, H, I, J)
              (E, F, G, H, I, J)
              (F, G, H, I, J)
              (G, H, I, J)
            and 3773 others
    = note: required for `bindings::qspinlock` to implement `pin_init::Zeroable`
    = note: 5 redundant requirements hidden
    = note: required for `drm_gem_shmem_object` to implement `pin_init::Zeroable`
note: required by a bound in `assert_zeroable`
   --> rust/kernel/lib.rs:243:23
    |
243 | fn assert_zeroable<T: pin_init::Zeroable>() {}
    |                       ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ required by this bound in `assert_zeroable`
fixed it now & pushed a TEST commit that checks if drm_gem_shmem_object implements Zeroable :)
oh cool! I'll check it out today or tomorrow
sorry I forgot to get to this! going to check it today
it works btw :)
thanks for checking :)
BTW - does this patch need any reviews?
ah I haven't sent this to the list yet
happy to help review when you do (I've also got some patch series that depend on it now)
just make sure to add me to the cc
or rather, the MaybeZeroable part already is there, only the buildsystem stuff is missing
sent: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20250520192307.259142-1-lossin@kernel.org/
I'm going over v2 of this patchset and noticed that I'm not actually implmenting Zeroable for Option<fn()>. I fixed this and then also replaced some usages of mem::zeroed that we already had.
but now my question is how many parameters do C functions have at a maximum in the kernel?
It's easy to add more and use a high number from the get-go, but I'd like to know if let's say 20 is a good starting number that would suffice for almost every function there is :)

https://elixir.bootlin.com/linux/v6.15-rc7/source/drivers/gpu/drm/amd/display/dc/dml/dcn314/display_mode_vba_314.c#L831

:)
okay let me get wc for that...
That is just in a C file, I was pointing it out for fun
yeah :)
the person who has to write abstractions for that will have to add a couple letters to my macro then :)
Really, the most I could find in include/ are about 15: https://elixir.bootlin.com/linux/v6.15-rc7/source/include/linux/ceph/osdmap.h#L275
